title: Template KNOT — Problem Statement
description: Template entry — replace with product-specific KNOTs

title: AAM/UAM network-defined operational concept
description: The AAM/UAM mission is defined by the network not the vehicle alone — vertiport pairs + airspace corridors + charging infrastructure + UTM integration constitute the certified system

title: U-space corridor operations definition
description: Operations confined to U-space corridor at 150–300 m AGL requiring USSP integration and UTM data exchange

title: Vertiport infrastructure pair requirement
description: Each mission requires a certified vertiport origin (A) and destination (B) with FATO pad ≥ 1.5D and ground charging infrastructure

title: Mission class — two altitude strata
description: Vehicle must operate across two altitude strata: ground-level vertiport operations (0–30 m) and U-space corridor cruise (150–300 m AGL)

title: Design range 30–150 km
description: Design range envelope covers 30–80 km metro missions and up to 150 km regional missions including energy reserves

title: Cruise performance 150–250 km/h TAS at 150–300 m AGL
description: Sustained cruise at 150–250 km/h TAS at 150–300 m AGL in U-space corridor under normal conditions

title: VTOL noise constraint ≤80 dB(A) at vertiport boundary
description: Noise level at vertiport boundary must not exceed 80 dB(A) during vertical take-off and landing

title: Passenger and payload envelope
description: Vehicle must accommodate single pilot or fully autonomous operation carrying 1–6 passengers with total payload 80–600 kg

title: MTOM range 700–3175 kg SC-VTOL compliant
description: Maximum Take-Off Mass must fall within 700–3175 kg bracket as defined by EASA SC-VTOL applicability scope

title: Energy reserves — 20 min VFR hold plus diversion
description: Vehicle must carry energy reserves sufficient for 20-minute VFR holding pattern plus diversion to an alternate vertiport or landing site

title: Dispatch target — 10 flights per vehicle per day
description: Operational target of minimum 10 revenue flights per vehicle per calendar day assuming 5–10 min turnaround at vertiport

title: Weather minima VFR-IFR with SC-VTOL Cat 1a maturity path
description: Initial operations under VFR minima evolving to IFR capability with SC-VTOL Cat 1a precision approach as fleet matures

title: Vertiport infrastructure footprint ≥1.5D FATO pad
description: Vertiport FATO must have minimum diameter of 1.5 × D where D equals 10 times the outer rotor envelope of the vehicle

title: Distributed electric propulsion architecture ATA 61+71
description: N motor-propulsor units on DC bus with motor-out certification logic; 4–18 rotor configurations with optional tilt capability; shared tech node with AMPEL360 Q100 BWB

title: Power management and peak buffering ATA 24
description: FC primary or battery-dominant primary power with battery buffer for transient loads; power management unit architected for fault tolerance

title: Battery thermal management and DPP ATA 24+96
description: Thermal runaway containment; SoH tracking with degradation model; Digital Product Passport per EU Battery Regulation; shared tech node
notes: Spillover to KNOT-Q100BWB-LC03-0003; A=0.50; key bidirectional node

title: DO-178C and DO-254 motor controller assurance
description: Motor controller software and hardware must achieve DAL A or DAL B certification for flight-critical electric drive; FBW and autonomous flight modes

title: First-of-kind certification pathway SC-VTOL
description: Certification under EASA SC-VTOL as first-of-kind type certificate; SC-VTOL precedent establishes basis for subsequent Q100 Special Conditions
notes: SC-VTOL TC application target 2026-12; feeds Q100 KNOT-Q100BWB-LC03-0005

title: Net-zero 100-passenger transport concept
description: Define operational concept for net-zero capable 100-passenger BWB transport aircraft using H2 FC primary power and distributed open-fan electric propulsion

title: Technology inheritance from AAM/UAM eVTOL programme
description: Capture technology nodes shared with AAM/UAM eVTOL programme and define KNOT spillover model (coefficient A=0.50) for bidirectional knowledge transfer

title: Distributed electric open-fan propulsion ATA 61+71
description: Distributed open-fan electric propulsion on DC bus with motor-out certification logic for BWB configuration; technology inherited from AAM/UAM eVTOL

title: H2 FC primary power management and peak buffering ATA 24
description: Hydrogen fuel cell primary power with battery buffer for transient peak loads; power management unit for fault-tolerant H2 FC + buffer integration

title: Buffer battery thermal management and DPP ATA 24+96
description: Cryogenic-compatible buffer battery thermal management; SoH tracking; Digital Product Passport per EU Battery Regulation 2023/1542
clause_refs: EU Battery Reg 2023/1542; CS-25 SC ATA 96
notes: Spillover from KNOT-AAMUAM-LC03-0003; A=0.50; key bidirectional node

title: DO-178C and DO-254 motor controller assurance with AI monitoring
description: Motor controller software DAL A/B per DO-178C and hardware DAL A/B per DO-254; AI-based health monitoring integration with FBW

title: CS-25 plus Special Conditions certification pathway
description: Certification under CS-25 + Special Conditions leveraging SC-VTOL type certificate precedent from AAM/UAM programme; target EASA type certificate 2031
notes: Depends on SC-VTOL precedent from KNOT-AAMUAM-LC03-0005
